note: Australian-produced. Grounded in the course's own modules. Founder to review before wiring live.

quizzes.title: Introduction to IAQ and Mould: Understanding Airborne Spread and Containment — Course Quiz

questions.questionText: According to the course, what does mould release that can circulate throughout a building and cause health problems when inhaled?
options: Microscopic spores
Carbon dioxide
Ground-level ozone
Volatile plasticisers
questions.sourceRef: M1 — The Impact of Mould on Indoor Air Quality (IAQ)

questions.questionText: Which toxic substances, produced by some moulds such as Stachybotrys chartarum, can negatively affect the immune system and contribute to respiratory illness when airborne?
options: Mycotoxins
Bacterial endotoxins
Nitrates
Chlorofluorocarbons
questions.sourceRef: M1 — The Impact of Mould on Indoor Air Quality (IAQ)

questions.questionText: The course identifies which factor as the most important in the spread of mould, with colonies growing faster and releasing more spores when it is high?
options: Moisture
Air temperature alone
Available light
The age of the building
questions.sourceRef: M2 — Understanding the Mechanisms of Mould Spread Through the Air

questions.questionText: Above which humidity level does the course say a perfect environment for mould growth is created?
questions.sourceRef: M2 — Understanding the Mechanisms of Mould Spread Through the Air

questions.questionText: How is negative pressure created in a mould-affected area so that spores stay contained during remediation?
options: Air scrubbers or negative air machines draw air out of the space so that air flows inward from uncontaminated areas
All windows are opened to increase natural airflow through the space
The building's HVAC system is set to its maximum fan speed
The affected room is heated well above normal room temperature
questions.sourceRef: M3 — Containment Strategies to Prevent Airborne Mould Spread

questions.questionText: Which Personal Protective Equipment does the course specify for restoration workers inside the contained zone?
options: Respirators equipped with HEPA filters, protective suits, gloves and goggles
Cloth face masks and cotton aprons
Ear protection and hard hats
Cotton gloves only
questions.sourceRef: M3 — Containment Strategies to Prevent Airborne Mould Spread

questions.questionText: During remediation, how should heavily contaminated materials such as drywall and carpeting be handled before disposal?
options: Sealed in plastic bags so spores are not released, then disposed of in accordance with local regulations
Left to dry in the open air before being carried out
Rinsed with water and returned to service
Stacked uncovered outside the containment barrier
questions.sourceRef: M4 — Remediation Techniques for Mould and Airborne Spores

questions.questionText: What is the purpose of post-remediation air testing described in the course?
options: To confirm that airborne spores have been removed and that spore levels are within safe limits
To measure the building's electricity consumption during works
To decide the market value of the property after restoration
To identify which brand of dehumidifier performs best
questions.sourceRef: M5 — Monitoring and Maintaining IAQ After Mould Remediation
